comments_1: Party A suggested joint venture arrangements and expressed interest in acquiring equity in the Company.
The Board approved the engagement of Greene Holcomb & Fisher LLC as the Company's investment banking firm.
comments_2: Company legal counsel was Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP; GHF's business was later acquired by BMO on 08/01/2016.

comments_1: Board decided to proceed with discussions with Party A and solicit interest from other potential third parties regarding a change in control transaction.
During the week of March 28, GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers, including Party A.
During the week of March 28, GHF contacted 18 potential financial buyers.
Party A was among the potential buyers that had executed confidentiality agreements.
G&W was among the potential buyers that had executed confidentiality agreements.
By 04/22/2016, Party B was included among the potential buyers that had executed confidentiality agreements.
Potential buyers were advised to submit non-binding indications of interest by 05/10/2016.
comments_2: The deadline was later postponed to 05/19/2016.

comments_1: Nine written IOIs were received between 05/19/2016 and 06/01/2016.
comments_2: Two low bidders were excluded from management presentations; seven bidders advanced.

comments_1: In mid-June, bidders were instructed to submit non-binding LOIs by 07/20/2016 together with mark-ups of the merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement.
In early July 2016, Party C approached management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company.
After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C received the memorandum concerning the Company.
Party C submitted a non-binding IOI.
G&W submitted a non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The proposal was subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period.
comments_3: The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a CVR, and G&W also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement, voting agreement and disclosure letter.

comments_1: Party B submitted a non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The proposal was subject to an expedited diligence review.
comments_3: Party B also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement.

comments_1: Party E submitted a non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The proposal was subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation.
comments_3: Party E provided only a summary of material issues and proposed changes to the merger agreement and voting agreement, rather than marked-up merger documents.

comments_1: Party D submitted a non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The proposal was subject to a four-week diligence period.
comments_3: Party D also provided mark-ups of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement.

comments_1: Party C submitted a non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The proposal was subject to a 30-day diligence period.

comments_1: Party F submitted a non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The proposal was subject to a 30-day due diligence period.

comments_1: Six LOIs were received in late July 2016.
G&W submitted a revised non-binding LOI in response to feedback that its prior price and CVR structure were not competitive.
comments_2: The revised offer still included cash plus a CVR.

comments_1: The Transaction Committee decided to proceed with G&W and Party B because of their higher offers and informed the remaining bidders, including Party C, that they were no longer involved in the process.

The Transaction Committee decided to proceed with G&W and Party B because of their higher offers and informed the remaining bidders, including Party F, that they were no longer involved in the process.

The Transaction Committee decided to proceed with G&W and Party B because of their higher offers and informed the remaining bidders, including Party D, that they were no longer involved in the process.

The Transaction Committee decided to proceed with G&W and Party B because of their higher offers and informed the remaining bidders, including Party E, that they were no longer involved in the process.
During a call with GHF, Party D expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing its offer.
During a call with GHF, Party E expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing its offer.
Party D submitted a revised non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The revised proposal contained a 30-day due diligence period.

comments_1: Party E submitted a revised non-binding LOI.
comments_2: The revised proposal included financing support from Party F and a 30-day due diligence period.

comments_1: After the Company declined to commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during Party D's 30-day diligence period, Party D said it would not proceed further at that time.
Party E withdrew its revised proposal on 08/02/2016 but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share.
comments_2: On 08/04/2016, the Company continued diligence and negotiations only with G&W and Party B.

comments_1: G&W submitted a revised non-binding LOI for $25.00 per share in cash; the prior CVR was removed.
comments_2: The revised LOI was submitted together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and voting agreement; a disclosure letter mark-up followed later that day.
comments_3: The offer stated that it would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.

comments_1: After being told another party had submitted a higher revised LOI, Party B said it would not increase its price above its prior proposal.
comments_2: The Board determined that the G&W offer represented the superior proposal.

comments_1: Merger agreement was executed shortly after Board approval; the voting agreement with the Eder Trusts was also executed.
Company publicly announced the transaction before the opening of trading on NASDAQ.
